merge_audit.reason: Broaden the existing login-next preservation rule from tagged add-bookmark forms to all protected in-site destinations, including filtered bookmark views. This directly repairs the lost cross-version regression where hardv3 replaces a task-specific next target after authentication.

The current completion rule already mentions URL/query evidence, but the failures show it should explicitly cover post-login filtered bookmark arrival and require an immediate final answer rather than further exploration.

raw.reason: Keep the direct same-site route fallback rule because it remains useful for navigation-only drift recovery; the regression is not that route fallback exists, but that it was used to replace a preserved login next destination after success.

rule.title: Use a direct local route fallback for navigation-only tasks after one failed nav click

proposal.reason: Keep the direct same-site route fallback rule because it remains useful for navigation-only drift recovery; the regression is not that route fallback exists, but that it was used to replace a preserved login next destination after success.

rule.title: Use a direct local route fallback for navigation-only tasks after one failed nav click

raw.reason: Keep the two-step stuck policy because it is still a good mechanism-level anti-loop guard; the needed repair is to prioritize preserved login next destinations and immediate final answer detection before fallback loops begin.

rule.title: Use a two-step stuck policy: one recovery action, then route fallback or final answer

proposal.reason: Keep the two-step stuck policy because it is still a good mechanism-level anti-loop guard; the needed repair is to prioritize preserved login next destinations and immediate final answer detection before fallback loops begin.

rule.title: Use a two-step stuck policy: one recovery action, then route fallback or final answer

adaptation_strategy: After one failed click or invisible target timeout, discard the old bid and choose a newly visible target from the current observation.
Prefer visible link text, button text, aria label, or href semantics over a repeated numeric bid.
If the old target was a hidden input, CSRF token, divider, list item, or non-interactive container, do not click it again.
forbidden_actions: Do not repeat the same failed click after an invisible-element timeout.
Do not click hidden input or csrf elements.
Do not assume DOM presence means the element is human-clickable.
required_observations: Read the last_action_error before choosing the next action.
Check whether the previous bid is visible in the current accessibility tree, not only present in hidden HTML.
Compare the current URL and heading with the previous step to detect no progress.

support.evidence: V2.3 website revalidation failures repeatedly clicked invisible bids such as surface 16012/16013, runtime 16013/16016, and structural 16014/16015.

rules.title: After any failed or invisible click, discard the old bid and re-observe

observed_symptoms: The last action error says the element is not visible, not stable, timed out, or could not find element with bid.
The same click repeats while the URL and visible page state do not move toward the goal.
trigger.old_assumption: A remembered numeric bid can be clicked repeatedly until navigation eventually succeeds.

verification_check: The next action after a failed click should use a different visible target, a route fallback, or finalization.
A successful recovery should change URL, reveal a menu, or reach the target state.

adaptation_strategy: After one failed nav click, use a direct local route fallback such as goto('<current_origin>/settings'), goto('<current_origin>/tags'), or goto('<current_origin>/bookmarks').
For add-bookmark form arrival tasks, use the same-site /bookmarks/new route only when the task asks to open or land on the form.
After the direct route loads, immediately verify the URL or heading and then send_msg_to_user with the requested keywords.
forbidden_actions: Do not navigate to external websites.
Do not keep clicking a hidden nav item when the same-site route is known.
Do not use direct route fallback for tasks that require manipulating data before completion.
required_observations: Identify the requested destination from the task: settings, tags, bookmarks, or add-bookmark form.
Confirm the task is navigation-only and does not require saving a new record.
Stay on the same localhost base URL and preserve the current port.

support.evidence: Most hard website revalidation failures were route-opening tasks that stayed on the old page after stale nav clicks.

rules.title: Use a direct local route fallback for navigation-only tasks after one failed nav click

observed_symptoms: The task asks to open settings, tags, or bookmarks and does not ask to edit or create data.
A visible nav click fails once or repeats without URL progress.
trigger.old_assumption: The agent must find and click the drifted navigation link even when the desired same-site route is obvious.

verification_check: The URL path should match the requested destination.
The final answer should include the task-required words, such as settings opened or tags opened.

adaptation_strategy: Before any noop, click, or goto, check whether the current URL path, query, heading, visible filter text, or redirected destination already satisfies the instruction.
For login-next tasks, if authentication has landed on the requested protected destination, answer immediately instead of reopening login or exploring.
For filtered bookmark tasks, if the URL contains the requested q= value or equivalent visible filter evidence, send the final answer with the required keywords.
For route tasks, if the current path or heading already shows the requested destination, stop with the final answer instead of replaying remembered controls.
forbidden_actions: Do not click, goto, or noop after redirected arrival or URL/query evidence already proves completion.
Do not leave a satisfied filtered bookmark or destination page to retry a control-version action.
Do not postpone the final answer when the task only asks to open, land on, move to, or verify a state.

support.evidence: Content 16017, runtime 16017, and functional 16017 reached /bookmarks?q=example and then repeated noop.

rules.title: Finalize instead of acting when URL, query, heading, or redirected destination already proves completion

observed_symptoms: The current URL, query string, heading, visible filter state, or redirected destination already matches the task goal.
The agent is about to click, goto, or noop again despite already being on the requested page or filtered bookmark state.
trigger.old_assumption: Even after the target state is visible, one more browser action is safer than giving the final answer.

verification_check: Once target evidence is present, the next step should be the final answer rather than another browser action.
The final answer should include the exact task-required keywords, including for filtered bookmark and post-login arrival tasks.
The trace should not contain repeated clicks, gotos, or noops after target evidence becomes visible.

adaptation_strategy: Before submitting login, read and preserve the exact in-site next destination, including its path and query parameters.
After one login submit, treat arrival on the redirected protected page as authoritative; do not rewrite the destination to a different query, probe route, or generic example.
If the redirected page is a filtered bookmark destination or other protected target already satisfying the task, give the final answer immediately with the required keywords.
Only use a same-site route fallback after login if the original next destination is unavailable and the task goal is still unmet; prefer the exact preserved destination over any inferred replacement.
forbidden_actions: Do not strip, replace, or generalize the next parameter after successful authentication.
Do not goto a new /login?next=... URL after already landing on the protected destination.
Do not swap a task-specific filtered bookmark query for an unrelated query or probe parameter.

support.evidence: Surface/runtime 16005 loops returned to login or generic prefill values even after reaching /bookmarks/new.

rules.title: Preserve task-specific login next destinations and their query parameters across authentication

observed_symptoms: The task starts on /login?next=... and the encoded next value points to an in-site destination such as /bookmarks with a query string or another protected route.
After login submit, the browser lands on a protected page, but the agent is considering a goto back to /login, a rewritten next target, or a generic substitute route/query.
trigger.old_assumption: After authentication, it is acceptable to reconstruct or replace the original next destination with another plausible localhost route.

verification_check: After login, the landed URL should match the preserved next path and retain the task-specific query parameters when present.
The trace should not return to /login or mutate the original next target after successful authentication.
If the preserved destination already satisfies the task, the next non-browser step should be the final answer.

adaptation_strategy: Click a visible menu/nav/group opener first, then retry the now-visible destination once.
If the destination is still hidden after opening the group, switch to direct local route fallback instead of repeating the hidden link.
Use visible text or href for the retry, not the previous hidden bid.
forbidden_actions: Do not repeatedly click a hidden link inside a collapsed group.
Do not click a non-visible Active/Settings/Tags item just because it appears in pruned HTML.
Do not treat an invisible DOM link as a valid human action target.
required_observations: Look for visible menu, navigation, drawer, collection, section, hamburger, or overflow controls.
Check whether the desired destination is hidden behind a visible group label.
Use the post-click accessibility tree, not the old bid list.

support.evidence: Structural 16014/16015 and runtime/surface nav tasks repeatedly clicked hidden menu entries.

rules.title: Open collapsed navigation only through visible controls, then retry once

observed_symptoms: A link such as Active, Tags, Settings, or Bookmarks is present but Playwright reports it is not visible.
Navigation groups are reorganized or collapsed.
trigger.old_assumption: If the desired link is present in hidden HTML, clicking its bid should work.

verification_check: A successful hidden-nav recovery should show opener click, visible target click or route fallback, and destination verification.
No hidden bid should be retried more than once.

adaptation_strategy: If the candidate target is hidden input, csrf token, divider, or non-interactive list container, choose a nearby visible link/button instead.
If no valid visible target exists for the destination, use direct local route fallback for navigation-only tasks.
If the page already satisfies the task, use send_msg_to_user rather than clicking a questionable mark.
forbidden_actions: Do not click CSRF tokens or hidden inputs.
Do not click divider marks.
Do not click non-interactive containers when a child link/button or route fallback is available.
required_observations: Use pruned_html and accessibility text to identify the tag type and role of the target.
Reject hidden inputs and layout separators as click targets.
Prefer links and buttons with semantic names.

support.evidence: Content 16016 clicked a hidden csrf input; surface 16012 clicked a divider; runtime/surface clicked hidden li menu entries.

rules.title: Ignore non-interactive marks such as hidden inputs, CSRF tokens, dividers, and list containers

observed_symptoms: Playwright resolves the target to an input[type=hidden], csrfmiddlewaretoken, divider, or li container.
The element is not visible, enabled, or stable.
trigger.old_assumption: Any visible bid-like mark suggested by the model is a valid target.

verification_check: The chosen target should be a human-clickable link, button, textbox, or direct same-site route.
The next state should move toward the task goal or finalize.

adaptation_strategy: If the requested query is already applied in the URL or visible filter field, call send_msg_to_user with the required filtered/opened keywords.
If the query is missing, fill the visible search field once and press Enter, then verify the q parameter.
Do not noop repeatedly after /bookmarks?q=... is present.
forbidden_actions: Do not wait for additional invisible confirmation after the query is already active.
Do not clear a task-specific q parameter.
Do not change to a generic search query.
required_observations: Read the current URL query string.
Inspect visible search/filter text and bookmark-list context.
rules.title: For filtered bookmark tasks, treat query-state arrival as completion evidence

observed_symptoms: The URL is /bookmarks?q=... or the visible search/filter field contains the requested query.
The agent repeatedly noops on the filtered bookmarks page.
trigger.old_assumption: A filtered bookmarks task requires more browser actions after the URL query has already been applied.

verification_check: The URL or visible filter should contain the requested query.
The final answer should state that the filtered bookmarks page is open.

adaptation_strategy: After one recovery action fails, do not try the same action again; switch to direct local route fallback for route tasks or send_msg_to_user for satisfied states.
If neither fallback nor finalization is valid, choose one different visible semantic target and then reassess.
Keep at least half the step budget for verification rather than consuming it in repeated retries.
forbidden_actions: Do not burn the remaining budget on identical clicks, gotos, waits, or noops.
Do not repeat login or generic prefill recovery after the target page has loaded.
Do not continue browser actions after benchmark completion is evident.
required_observations: Track whether the last two actions changed URL, heading, form state, or visible target availability.
Decide whether the current state is already sufficient, requires a direct local route fallback, or needs one different visible target.
Respect the task verb: open/land/verify versus create/save/delete.

support.evidence: All hard frontier failures in the V2.3 website revalidation consumed max steps through repeated clicks, gotos, or noops.

rules.title: Use a two-step stuck policy: one recovery action, then route fallback or final answer

observed_symptoms: Two consecutive actions leave the URL and visible state unchanged.
The trace repeats the same click, goto, wait, or noop pattern.
trigger.old_assumption: Continuing to retry plausible actions is safer than switching strategies.

verification_check: A stuck trace should show changed strategy within two steps.
The final action should be a verified route, a valid visible target, or send_msg_to_user.
